other_observers: Mymm Ackley, Coen Dexter, Brenda Wright; Nick Komar found the bird on Saturday. Many others have seen it.
specific_location: S end of Duck Lake, near Fossil Creek Reservoir.
description: Overall body bulk approx. equivalent to that of adjacent Green-winged Teal. Roughly twice the body length and depth, back to belly, of adjacent Stilt Sandpiper. Seems proportionately larger-bodied and smaller-headed than dowitchers. Bill long, approx. 2.5x length of head, very slightly upturned, basal half reddish or orange, distal half dark. Legs appear dark. Belly brick-red; wings and back seem grayer, mottled with some buff. Neck and head paler grayish.Behaviors: Bird was feeding actively in a sewing machine fashion, a la dowitchers, just off the shore in 4-5 inches of water.Call: Bird was silent.Plumage: adult alternate
